etymology.description: phonetic 䜌 + semantic 心 ('heart') -> ties of the heart -> romance
etymology.interpretation: 戀(lì) represents 'romance' by combining 心(xīn, 'heart') as the semantic element indicating emotions, with 䜌 as the phonetic component suggesting the sound 'liàn'. The heart tied up or entangled captures the sense of romantic attachment and longing.
etymology.simplification: 戀(戀) was simplified to 恋(戀) by removing the phonetic 䜌 and retaining only the semantic 心(xīn, 'heart').

examples.chinese: 他们的恋情很浪漫(他們的戀情很浪漫)。
她在校园里与他相恋(她在校園裡與他相戀)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài xiàoyuán lǐ yǔ tā xiāngliàn.
examples.english: She fell in love with him on campus.

examples.chinese: 他一直希望找到一份真诚的爱情(他一直希望找到一份真誠的愛情)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yīzhí xīwàng zhǎodào yī fèn zhēnchéng de àiqíng.
examples.english: He has always hoped to find a sincere love.
